records.title: DHS AEL 03OE-07-SUAS — DFR Drone Systems Grant Eligibility
records.summary: DHS Authorized Equipment List item 03OE-07-SUAS covers DFR drone-in-a-box systems eligible for HSGP, BSIR, and COPS Technology grant reimbursement. NDAA and ASDA compliance required. Blue UAS listing not mandatory but simplifies procurement. Related items: 03OE-07-UASC (controllers), 07CP-01-DRON (communications payloads). Verify current AEL before procurement — updated annually.

records.title: FAA BEYOND Waiver Program — 214 Approved, Avg 7 Days, Part 108 Mid-2026
records.summary: FAA BEYOND program status: 214+ waivers approved as of June 2025. Average approval time under 7 days (down from 11+ months in 2024). Fastest approval: under 2 hours. 6x YoY DFR program growth. Required equipment: parachute system, anti-collision lighting, detect-and-avoid (DAA). Part 108 national BVLOS framework expected mid-2026, replacing case-by-case waivers. 58 of 78 pending waivers stalled due to incomplete applications.

data.part_108_description: Will replace case-by-case waivers with standardized national BVLOS framework
data.notes: Data sourced from Police1 / DRONERESPONDERS June 2025 reporting. Update when FAA publishes new aggregate figures.

FAA BEYOND Waiver Program — Status Snapshot
records.summary: FAA BEYOND program: 214+ waivers approved as of June 2025. Average approval time under 7 days (down from 11+ months in 2024). Fastest approval: under 2 hours. 6x YoY DFR program growth. Part 108 national BVLOS framework expected mid-2026, replacing case-by-case waivers. Required equipment: parachute, anti-collision lighting, detect-and-avoid (DAA). 58 of 78 pending waivers stalled due to incomplete applications.

records.title: DHS AEL 03OE-07-SUAS — DFR Grant Eligibility Reference
records.summary: AEL item 03OE-07-SUAS covers DFR drone-in-a-box systems eligible for DHS grant reimbursement under HSGP, BSIR, and COPS Technology. NDAA and ASDA compliance required. Blue UAS not mandatory but simplifies procurement. Related items: 03OE-07-UASC (controllers), 07CP-01-DRON (communications payloads). AEL is updated annually — verify current eligibility before procurement.

records.title: FCC Conditional Approvals — 4 Drone Systems Approved Through Dec 31 2026
records.summary: FCC March 18 2026: first Conditional Approvals for drone systems under new framework. 4 systems approved through December 31 2026. New compliance pathway alongside Blue UAS and Green UAS — does NOT replace NDAA requirements. Conditional Approval is system-specific, not manufacturer-wide. All 4 expire Dec 31 2026. Grant eligibility matrices must be updated to reflect this pathway.

records.title: ASDA + OMB M-26-02 — Federal DJI Fleet Restriction Timeline
records.summary: Complete ASDA restriction timeline: FY2024 NDAA/ASDA signed Dec 2023 — two years notice. OMB M-26-02 issued Nov 21 2025 — implementing ASDA provisions effective Dec 22 2025. FCC Covered List action Dec 22 2025 — all foreign-made UAS blocked from new equipment authorization. FHWA guidance prohibits DJI on federally-funded transportation projects. Combined effect: any agency using federal grants (COPS, HSGP, FHWA, BRIC) cannot procure or operate DJI. Phase-out of existing fleets required by Dec 2027.

records.title: FAA Part 108 — National BVLOS Framework Expected Mid-2026
records.summary: FAA Deputy Executive Director Paul Strande at DRONERESPONDERS National Conference (March 10 2026, Williamsburg VA): Part 108 will replace case-by-case BEYOND waivers with a standardized national framework for BVLOS operations. Expected finalization mid-2026. Shift signals the FAA is treating DFR as infrastructure, not exception. Agencies that get BEYOND waivers now will be better positioned for Part 108 compliance.

records.title: Oregon/NASAO White Paper — 467 Airframes Grounded, $50M–$2B National Exposure
records.summary: Oregon/NASAO Revision 2 white paper: 25 state transportation departments surveyed, 467 airframes grounded across 23 states. National exposure $50M–$2B. Replacement cost $20–60K per airframe vs $15K DJI equivalent (2–4x premium). Idaho benchmark: $15K drone → $42K compliant. States with zero replacement budget: OR, GA, WI, IN, MN, NE, ID, NY. Waiver until September 2027 recommended. Triggered by OMB M-26-02, FHWA guidance, FCC Covered List (all Dec 22 2025).
records.paywall_reason: Paid — state-by-state breakdown is the premium data product

records.title: Skydio DFR Command — 10M+ Calls, 25+ Integrations, NG911 Pre-CAD Dispatch
records.summary: Skydio DFR Command: 10M+ calls for service processed (Feb 2026). 25+ public safety system integrations including CAD, NG911, gunshot detection, ALPR, body-worn cameras. NG911 integration dispatches drone before call enters CAD — cutting response by several minutes. CJIS-compliant evidence chain with auto-upload to Axon Evidence. 3,800+ agency deployments. Brookhaven PD: under 60 seconds, drone on scene while still typing the call.

records.title: BRINC Guardian — First Starlink DFR Drone, NLC Exclusive, 900+ Agencies
records.summary: BRINC Guardian launched March 24 2026 — world's first Starlink-connected 911 response drone. 900+ agency deployments, 25%+ of US SWAT teams. National League of Cities named BRINC exclusive drone partner reaching 2,600+ member cities. Blue UAS listed. Seattle manufacturing. Most capable 911 drone per BRINC's own marketing. Adds to Lemur 2 product line for indoor/tactical use.

records.title: Versaterm Acquires Aloft — Single-Stack CAD + Fleet + LAANC Consolidation
records.summary: Versaterm acquired DroneSense (July 2025) then Aloft (Feb 2026). Aloft powers majority of US LAANC authorizations. Combined entity: DroneSense fleet management + Aloft airspace authorization + Versaterm CAD in single vendor. Agencies can now dispatch drones from CAD exactly like patrol/fire/EMS units. Major DFR software market consolidation. Raises single-vendor dependency risk for agencies.
records.paywall_reason: Paid — acquisition/vendor intel is Gray Zone tier

records.title: Ohio Statewide DFR Pilot — First Participants, SkyfireAI Managing Under ODOT
records.summary: Ohio identified first municipal participants in nation's only statewide DFR pilot. SkyfireAI program manager under ODOT/DriveOhio. Pre-approved vendor list (closed Jan 1 2026): Skydio, BRINC, Parrot. Reimbursement model: agencies pay upfront, state reimburses. Typical package $110–230K per site. Texas, California, Florida, Michigan watching as template. CAL Analytics handling UTM coordination.

records.title: NLC + BRINC National DFR Initiative — 2,600+ Member Cities
records.summary: National League of Cities and BRINC launched national DFR initiative in Washington DC. BRINC is exclusive NLC drone partner. Focus: education, direct engagement with local officials, implementation guidance. NLC membership 2,600+ cities — functions as quasi-distribution channel for BRINC municipal procurement. Every NLC city that adopts DFR is a BRINC procurement signal.

records.title: Seiler GeoDrones — Wisconsin 100% Grounded, No NDAA Pivot, Highest Midwest Exposure
records.summary: Seiler Instrument GeoDrones (est. 1945, St. Louis MO, 9 Midwest states) is authorized DJI dealer still featuring DJI Matrice 400 as flagship as of April 2026 with no visible NDAA pivot. Wisconsin — their primary state — is 100% grounded per NASAO white paper. Indiana 85% ($400K at risk), Nebraska 86%, Illinois moderate. Known at-risk WI programs: Manitowoc County Sheriff (13 FAA-cert pilots), Beaver Dam PD, Wisconsin Rapids PD. Seiler customers hold stranded DJI assets with no replacement guidance from their incumbent dealer. Highest Forge GTM priority in the Midwest.
records.paywall_reason: Paid — proprietary dealer analysis, actionable GTM intel

records.title: Frontier Precision — Most Advanced Trimble Dealer NDAA Pivot, OR/CO/MN Severely Exposed
records.summary: Frontier Precision (est. 1988, Bismarck ND, employee-owned, 22+ states, 9 Trimble Certified Service Centers) is the most advanced of the three Trimble dealers in transitioning to NDAA-compliant platforms. Carrying Skydio, WISPR, Inspired Flight, ACSL SOTEN, Freefly, Quantum-Systems alongside DJI. Primary state exposures per NASAO: Oregon 95% grounded (Frontier has Portland/Tigard service center, OR authored the white paper), Colorado 90% (Arvada + Colorado Springs), Minnesota 84% (Minneapolis hub). Channel partner candidate — already advising customers on transition, Forge compliance intelligence layer is additive.

records.title: Duncan-Parnell — Southeast Exposure, Florida 2022 State Ban Unresolved, Georgia SEVERE
records.summary: Duncan-Parnell (est. 1946, Charlotte NC, 20+ locations, exclusive Trimble dealer TN, 8 Trimble Certified Service Centers) has 20+ Southeast branches. Georgia 80% grounded $225K (Norcross GA branch). North Carolina probable high exposure — HQ state, major DOT infrastructure spend, not in NASAO sample. Florida: state DJI ban April 2023 grounded $200M+ in drones, state provided only $25M replacement funding. Jacksonville and Orlando branches. Public safety UAS division covers GA, FL, Carolinas, VA. Partial NDAA pivot underway.

records.title: UVT — 2,400+ Agency Relationships, Pure-Play DFR, Channel Partner Candidate
records.summary: Unmanned Vehicle Technologies (est. 2014, Fayetteville AR, founded by 911 dispatcher Chris Fink) serves 2,400+ government agencies nationwide. Tier I DJI Enterprise Dealer. TIPS Contract #240101, Sourcewell #011223-UNM. No Trimble dependency — pure drone focus. Active NDAA pivot. DFR program page with FAA waiver support, site selection, SOP development. Nebraska 86% grounded (UVT active). Highest-leverage channel partner candidate: UVT has the 2,400 agency relationships, Forge has the compliance intelligence data layer. Explore white-label or referral arrangement with Chris Fink directly.

records.title: Advexure — NDAA Content Leader, Free Tier Competitor, Not a Channel Partner
records.summary: Advexure (national e-commerce, one of North America's largest UAS dealers/distributors) is producing comprehensive NDAA/Blue UAS/Green UAS compliance content that competes with Forge's free tier on search. Strong NDAA pivot — carrying ACSL SOTEN, Inspired Flight, Wingtra, Freefly, WISPR alongside DJI. Public safety drone page active. Not a channel partner candidate — building the same content layer. Differentiate Forge on proprietary data: NASAO state records, grant eligibility matrices, confidence scoring, live miner pipeline vs editorial content.

records.title: DSLRPros — Most Sophisticated DFR Content Competitor, Correct Nuance on DJI Grounding
records.summary: DSLRPros (national e-commerce) is producing the most sophisticated DJI transition and DFR compliance content in the dealer ecosystem. Long-form pieces including DJI Ban 2025 Complete Guide, Are You Prepared, What the FCC Update Means for Police DFR Leaders. Correctly tells agencies DJI aircraft are NOT grounded in 2026 — exposure is replacement timing not flight authority. Most direct Forge content competitor at the free tier. Differentiator: Forge has proprietary data pipeline (NASAO records, grant matrices, BEYOND data, live miners) vs DSLRPros editorial content.

records.title: Dearborn MI — Full-Scale Skydio DFR, First in Michigan, Public Transparency Dashboard
records.summary: Dearborn Michigan launched full-scale Skydio DFR February 2026, first in Michigan. Covers any point in city within 2.5 minutes. Use cases: violent crimes, traffic crashes, missing persons, vehicle break-ins. Flight data publicly available via transparency dashboard. Also used for: infrastructure surveys, storm damage assessment, FEMA documentation. Force multiplier model — clears calls without sending officers.

records.title: San Bernardino County CA — Active DFR Program
records.summary: San Bernardino County Sheriff active DFR program. One of the larger county-level programs in California. Part of the growing California DFR adoption wave. California DOT 30% impacted per NASAO (91 airframes) — county programs may have separate fleet exposure.

records.title: Sterling Heights MI PD — Active DFR Program, $678K COPS Tech Grant 2026
records.summary: Sterling Heights Michigan Police Department active DFR program. Received $678,822 COPS Technology grant (5-year) in 2026 — one of the largest COPS DFR awards documented this cycle. Michigan is active DFR state — Dearborn is first full-scale, Sterling Heights further validates the market.

records.title: Everdrone — EU Medical DFR Leader, AED Delivery, UK £20M Government Investment
records.summary: Everdrone (Gothenburg Sweden) is the global leader in drone emergency medical services. AED delivery drones integrated into emergency response chain alongside ambulances. Operations in Västra Götaland + Stockholm (Sweden), Normandy (France via SAMU), UK (KSS Air Ambulance, Project Lifeline). SEK 36M raised March 2026. UK government announced £20M+ package to accelerate medical drone and NHS integration. Clinical trial with Karolinska Institutet 2026. Key metric: drone arrives before ambulance in 64% of OHCA cases, median 2-3 min advantage.

records.title: [EXCLUSIVE] Wisconsin 100% Grounded — Seiler Void, Known Programs, Grant Path Forward
records.summary: Wisconsin DOT confirmed 100% fleet grounded — worst in 25-state NASAO survey. No dedicated replacement budget. Seiler GeoDrones still featuring DJI Matrice 400 as flagship (April 2026), no NDAA pivot. At-risk programs: Manitowoc County Sheriff (13 FAA-cert pilots, 2022 launch), Beaver Dam PD (active thermal), Wisconsin Rapids PD. Replacement cost: $15K DJI → $42K compliant (Idaho benchmark, 2.8x premium). Grant path: COPS Technology ($50K–750K+, annual Q1-Q2), HSGP/SHSP via Wisconsin Emergency Management, UASI for Milwaukee metro. 72 counties all likely exposed — state DOT number (1 airframe) massively understates true statewide exposure.

records.title: US drone future hinges on policy, investment, coordination
records.summary: The Commercial Drone Alliance is pushing Washington to move faster, and more strategically, on building a truly domestic drone ecosystem. In a newly released white paper, the industry group calls on the Trump Administration to adopt sweeping reforms aimed at strengthening US manufacturing, creating high-skilled jobs, and reducing reliance on foreign-made drone components. The message is clear: recent policy moves have set the tone, but now the US needs a coordinated, long-term plan to stay compe

records.title: America’s new 911 drone is always ready
records.summary: Drones responding to 911 calls are no longer experimental. Across the US, police departments are already using Drone as First Responder (DFR) programs to get eyes on emergencies faster than patrol units. In many cities, drones arrive in under two minutes, well before officers. But even with that progress, the tech still has friction. Drones need to stop and recharge. Coverage areas are limited. Connectivity can fail in the worst moments. Now, BRINC says its new Guardian drone is built to fix tho

records.title: US Army orders 2,500 Skydio drones in record deal
records.summary: The US Army just made one thing very clear: small drones aren’t just helpful anymore; they’re essential. In a record-setting move, Skydio has secured an order worth more than $52 million to supply over 2,500 of its X10D drones to the Army. That makes it the largest small drone procurement from a single manufacturer in Army history — and perhaps even more surprising, the entire process from bid to award wrapped up in under 72 hours.more…

records.title: 10 million calls: The drone system changing policing
records.summary: How fast can a drone respond to a 911 call? In some departments, the answer is under 60 seconds, and the software making that possible just passed 10 million processed calls.more…

records.title: New US-made drone Alpha aims to redefine first response
records.summary: Atlanta-based Flock Safety has unveiled Alpha, a US-built, NDAA-compliant drone designed to help law enforcement reach emergencies faster and with sharper situational awareness. The company says Alpha represents a major step forward in Drone as First Responder (DFR) technology, combining speed, endurance, and precision in one compact system.more…

records.title: This US state is letting drones answer 911 calls, and it could change everything
records.summary: What if your next 911 call was answered by a drone? Ohio has just launched the nation’s first statewide Drone First Responder (DFR) program, a two-year pilot designed to test how drones can reach emergency scenes faster, provide live aerial views to dispatchers, and ultimately help save lives.more…

records.title: Arlington expands drone program to speed up police response
records.summary: Arlington, Texas is stepping up its crime-fighting game from the skies.The Arlington Police Department (APD) has announced a major expansion of its Drone as a First Responder (DFR) program, designed to get officers real-time information before they even arrive on scene. The idea is simple but powerful: when a 911 call comes in, APD drone pilots can instantly launch a drone to the location, giving officers a live aerial view of what’s happening while they’re still en route.more…

records.title: Drones now map emergencies before crews hit the ground
records.summary: In a major tech upgrade for emergency response teams, SkyeBrowse and Paladin have partnered to bring real-time 3D modeling to Drone as First Responder (DFR) programs — eliminating delays and giving first responders eyes on the scenebeforethey arrive.more…

records.title: Emergency drones now launch from 911 calls instantly with Flock
records.summary: Flock Safety is taking emergency response to new heights — literally. The Atlanta-based tech company just announced a game-changing update: drones that launch the second a 911 call comes in. It’s called Flock911 for Aerodome, and it’s officially the first system in the US that allows police drones to deploy based on a live 911 call — before it’s even entered into dispatch.more…

records.title: Extreme drone training? Flying Lion’s FLI Town has it covered
records.summary: First responders looking to improve their drone skills just got a groundbreaking new training ground. Drone service providerFlying Lionhas unveiled FLI Town, the nation’s first dedicated Drone as First Responder (DFR) training center. This state-of-the-art facility is designed for police, fire, and emergency teams to sharpen their drone skills in real-world scenarios — all in a safe and controlled environment.more…

records.title: No spotters, no limits: California police drones now fly at 400 feet
records.summary: California’s Elk Grove Police Department (EGPD) is officially soaring to new heights — literally. The Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) has granted EGPD a groundbreaking Beyond Visual Line of Sight (BVLOS) waiver, making them the first law enforcement agency in the US to fly drones at 400 feet across an entire city — without human spotters.more…

records.title: Skydio DFR study shows why police rely on drones
records.summary: US drone makerSkydiohas unveiled the nation’s first comprehensive study on Drone as First Responder (DFR) operations, shedding light on how drones are transforming public safety. The study analyzed 1,779 flights across various jurisdictions — from bustling metropolitan areas to quiet rural counties — between September 15 and November 14, 2024. The findings highlight the pivotal role drones play in enhancing law enforcement efficiency, ensuring officer and community safety, and providing critical

records.title: How Flying Lion’s drones lead first responders to safer outcomes
records.summary: Drone service provider Flying Lion has marked an impressive milestone in public safety innovation. By the end of 2024, the organization completed 55,773 Drone as First Responder (DFR) flights, with over 35,000 conducted Beyond Visual Line of Sight (BVLOS). These flights form part of a growing national effort to enhance emergency response and community safety.more…

records.title: Flock Safety scoops up Aerodome, boosting US-made drones
records.summary: Drones as First Responder (DFR) tech specialist Aerodome has been acquired by Flock Safety, a company known for its public safety tech. The move brings Aerodome’s advanced drone capabilities into Flock Safety’s portfolio, aiming to make emergency responses quicker and safer for law enforcement.more…

records.title: New Skydio software solution for drone emergency response
records.summary: Drone manufacturer Skydio has launched DFR Command, a software solution that aims to simplify how public safety agencies deploy drones in emergency situations. According to the company, it’s the world’s first software solution built to start, operate, and grow Drone as First Responder (DFR) programs.more…

records.title: First responders gain BVLOS drone advantage with BRINC, Echodyne collab
records.summary: Public safety drone technology specialist BRINC has partnered with advanced radar solutions provider Echodyne to push the boundaries of drone operations for emergency services. This collaboration aims to enable fully automated drone operations beyond visual line of sight (BVLOS) without the need for visual observers — a current requirement under FAA regulations.more…

records.title: Axon launches Drone as First Responder solution with Skydio
records.summary: Axon, the company that invented Taser stun guns, is joining forces with drone maker Skydio to deliver a scalable Drone as a First Responder (DFR) solution.more…

records.title: Aerodome raises $21.5M to expand drone use in emergency response
records.summary: Drones as First Responder (DFR) tech specialist Aerodome has secured $21.5 million in Series A funding to enhance its solutions and expand its engineering and go-to-market teams. The funding round, led by CRV with contributions from Andreessen Horowitz, Karman Ventures, Immad Akhund, and Ford Street Ventures, brings Aerodome’s total funding to $28 million since its seed round in October 2023.more…

records.title: This 911 response drone can reach call locations within seconds
records.summary: US drone maker BRINC has unveiled a new nested drone solution designed specifically for Drone as First Responder (DFR) operations. The company says its Responder drone can be auto-dispatched from its charging station in under 5 seconds, which allows it to arrive at a 911 call location in less than 70 seconds.more…

records.title: BRINC, Skyfire partner to advance drones as first responders
records.summary: Public safety technology developer BRINC is joining forces with drone consulting company Skyfire to provide regulatory support, equipment, software, and training to law enforcement and emergency response agencies deploying drones.more…

records.title: This tool improves police drone accountability, fosters public trust
records.summary: Drone fleet management platform AirData says it is encouraging law enforcement transparency by making more public-facing features available to community-oriented police departments across the US. The company’s Public Portal program boosts public trust by improving accountability in drone use.more…

records.title: Skyfire provides turnkey DFR outsourcing to public safety agencies
records.summary: Drone public safety and emergency program consultancySkyfireis taking a major step beyond its habitual advisory activities to police and fire departments by offering its own dronefirst responder(DFR) services as an outsourced solution to assembling in-house UAV units.more…

records.title: Chula Vista PD — Founding DFR Program, 4,303 Calls Without Dispatch
records.summary: Chula Vista PD launched October 2018 — first-ever active emergency BVLOS waiver for public safety (March 2019). 5 launch sites, city-wide coverage. First program to obtain launch-from-anywhere authorization (March 2021) and first 2:1 waiver (two drones per launch site). BVLOS radius 3 miles from any launch site. Key metric: 4,303 calls handled by drone without dispatching a patrol unit. Chief Kennedy: 'DFR has become one of our best de-escalation tools.' FAA Integration Pilot Program origin — 1 of 10 nationally selected. Notable: EFF found ~1-in-10 flights lacked stated purpose; robust transparency policy essential.

key_facts.milestone: First-ever active emergency BVLOS waiver for public safety

records.title: Fort Wayne PD — First Part 107 DFR Waiver Without Visual Observer
records.summary: Fort Wayne PD launched January 2022. 12 pilots total, 2 dedicated to DFR. Dispatch trigger: Live911 monitoring — pilots detect calls benefiting from aerial overwatch. FAA milestone: first Part 107 DFR waiver WITHOUT visual observer requirement (Class G airspace, up to 200' AGL). Pending upgrade: pre-approved for full Part 91 + Part 107 BVLOS up to 400' pending IRIS Automation IRISSHIELD DAA sensor installation. Covers law enforcement, fire monitoring, EMS support. Notable de-escalation case: drone identified weapon-call subject as train enthusiast with radio — no officers exposed to unnecessary risk.

key_facts.milestone: First Part 107 DFR waiver without visual observer

records.title: Campbell PD — First 24/7 DFR Without Visual Observer, Dedrone DedroneBeyond
records.summary: Campbell PD (CA) DFR started 2022 daytime with human VO under FR-BVLOS waiver. October 2024 milestone: first-ever FAA waiver for 24/7 DFR operations WITHOUT visual observer. Uses Dedrone DedroneBeyond ground-based radar/sensor fusion DAA system (9km validated range). Authority: 14 CFR 91.113(b) Part 91 COW, up to 400' AGL. Remote pilot at control center; DedroneBeyond acts as 'virtual visual observer.' DAA independently validated to ASTM F3442M-23 by Virginia Tech MAAP. FAA indicated data from this trial will shape replication process for future 24/7 no-VO applicants. First demonstration that radar+AI sensor fusion can substitute for human VO at night.

key_facts.validation: ASTM F3442M-23 by Virginia Tech MAAP

records.title: Paladin Knighthawk — Commercial DFR Pioneer, $30K-$300K/yr, Fully Automated Dispatch
records.summary: Paladin (formerly Emergency Response Software) Knighthawk drone + Watchtower software. One of few companies granted TBVLOS waiver. ~1,600 emergency responses since 2018. Operating in Memorial Villages and Houston TX, Orange Township OH. Fully automated dispatch via Watchtower from 911 call coordinates. Typical drone response: 1-2 minutes vs 10 minutes for ground unit. Pricing: approximately $30,000-$300,000 annually (case-by-case agency pricing). Now partnered with SkyeBrowse for 3D structure mapping before crew arrival — fire/EMS use case expanding DFR beyond LE.

key_facts.dispatch: Fully automated via Watchtower from 911 coords

records.title: Part 108 BVLOS Rulemaking — Transition Risk for Existing COW Holders
records.summary: Part 108 NPRM (FAA-2025-1908) published August 7 2025 — 700+ pages, 3,000+ public comments by October 6 2025 close. Final rule target: spring 2026 per EO 14307 (240-day mandate). Implementation: 6-12 months after final rule (late 2026-early 2027). Max UAS weight: 1,320 lbs. Two pathways: Operating Permit (24-month, lower complexity) and Operating Certificate (ongoing, full SMS required). Key risk: Part 108 proposes eliminating new Part 107 BVLOS waivers once final rule takes effect. Industry pushing for grandfathering. Existing COWs remain valid during interim. Agencies should begin Part 108 documentation now: SMS, training program, organizational policies.

records.title: FAA COW Program Metrics — 214 Approved, 300 Submitted, 2-4 Week Turnaround
records.summary: As of June 11 2025: 300 COW submissions, 214 approved, 78 pending (58 requiring agency action due to incomplete submissions). Typical approval: 2-4 weeks (vs 10-12 months under old COA). COW valid 48 months. 87% of applicants choose 200' Shielded Ops; 13% choose 400' DAA. Compared to pre-2024: ~50 total DFR waivers approved across six years. Ground units returned to service before drone arrival: 25% average across DFR programs. Law enforcement agencies using drones: 1,400+ (54% increase in 6 years as of 2023). Old TBVLOS/FR-BVLOS types no longer accepted for new applicants — unified 91.113 COW replaced all.

data.part_108_description: Will replace case-by-case waivers with standardized national BVLOS framework
data.notes: Data sourced from Police1 / DRONERESPONDERS June 2025 reporting. Update when FAA publishes new aggregate figures.
